---
title: MCP tool reference
description: The tools your agent can call over anima-mcp.
---

Once your agent is [paired and connected](/build/quickstart), it has six tools against your vault. Every write is signed with your agent key and attributed by `ANIMA_AGENT_NAME`.

## recall

Search the vault and return the most relevant notes, decrypted from Walrus.

- **Input:** `query` (string). Keywords, a topic, or a question.
- **Returns:** a list of notes, each with `noteId`, `title`, `body`, `tags`, and `updatedAt`.
- **No match:** `No memories matched "<query>".`

## remember

Write a new note into the vault. It is encrypted, stored on Walrus, and attributed to this agent. The first write takes ten to twenty seconds.

- **Input:** `title` (string), `body` (markdown string), `tags` (optional string array).
- **Returns:** a confirmation with the new note id, the author, and the Walrus blob ids (quilt and blob object).
- **On failure:** a readable error if the agent key is unpaired or cannot afford the write (testnet SUI/WAL), never a stack trace.

## edit_note

Write a new version of an existing note. This is durable, correctable memory: the new version is encrypted on Walrus and attributed to this agent. Use it to correct or extend what you know about the owner. Takes ten to twenty seconds.

- **Input:** `noteId` (string). A ULID from `recall` or `list_notes`. Plus any of `title` (string), `body` (markdown string), `tags` (string array), `links` (string array). Omit a field to keep the current value; `tags` and `links` replace the current set.
- **Returns:** a confirmation with the note id, the new version number, the author, and the new Walrus blob ids.
- **Unknown id:** `Note <id> not found in the vault: list_notes to see valid ids, or use remember to create one.`

## list_notes

List every note in the vault: id, title, tags, last update, and author.

- **Input:** none.
- **Returns:** one line per note.
- **Empty vault:** `The vault is empty.`

## read_note

Return the full markdown of a single note, including its frontmatter.

- **Input:** `noteId` (string). A ULID from `recall` or `list_notes`.
- **Returns:** the full markdown of the note, including its frontmatter.
- **Unknown id:** `No note with id <id>: use list_notes or recall to find valid ids.`

## place_note

Position a note on the multiplayer canvas at (x, y). This updates the durable canvas layout, which is a Walrus write.

- **Input:** `noteId` (string, a ULID from `recall` or `list_notes`), `x` (number), `y` (number), `canvasId` (optional string; defaults to the shared board).
- **Returns:** a confirmation that the note was placed at (x, y), with the number of notes the canvas layout now covers.
- **Unknown id:** `Note <id> not found in the vault: list_notes to see valid ids.`

<Callout type="info">
`place_note` is shipped, but it does not yet carry the same end-to-end live verification as `recall`, `remember`, and `read_note`. One edge case: the first time an agent writes the shared board, the save can hand back an owner-signed delete for the legacy layout blob. The agent key cannot sign that wallet-owned delete, so it is dropped, and the layout self-heals on the owner's next board write from the frontend.
</Callout>
